title: Living Lands Charter Internships 2026 (x2) at the Commonwealth Secretariat – Paid Climate Change Internship in London
summary: Looking for a paid climate change internship in the UK ? Applications are now open for the Living Lands Charter Internships (x2) at the Commonwealth Secretariat , offering young professionals the opportunity to work on global climate, biodiversity, and sustainable land management initiatives. This six-month internship provides hands-on experience within an international policy environment while supporting implementation of the Commonwealth Living Lands Charter , a major global commitment adopted at the Commonwealth Heads of Government Meeting (CHOGM) in Kigali, Rwanda. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); About the Living Lands Charter Internship The Living Lands Charter Internship sits within the Climate Change Section of the Climate Change and Oceans Division at the Commonwealth Secretariat in London. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); The Commonwealth Living Lands Charter was adopted in June 2022 and represents a shared commitment by all 56 Commonwealth member countries to: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Address climate change, biodiversity loss, and land degradation Promote climate-resilient and sustainable land management Strengthen global collaboration on environmental protection Support inclusive and evidence-based policy solutions Interns will gain exposure to international development, environmental governance, and climate policy while contributing to real-world initiatives impacting countries across the Commonwealth. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Internship Details Position: Living Lands Charter Intern (2 positions) Duration: 6 months Location: London, United Kingdom Salary: 498.60 per week Organisation: Commonwealth Secretariat This internship offers a professional international environment where selected candidates will build networks, develop technical skills, and gain valuable experience in climate and environmental policy. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Key Responsibilities Under the supervision of the Adviser Climate Change, interns will: Support research across key Living Lands Charter themes including: Agriculture Water and soil conservation Climate and health Biodiversity and green development Indigenous peoples and sustainable land use Monitor media and academic developments related to climate change and land management Help promote awareness and visibility of the Living Lands Charter Support updates to databases and the Living Lands micro-website Contribute to policy and governance research related to climate and biodiversity financing and capacity building ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Eligibility Criteria To apply, candidates must: Be 20 30 years old Be currently enrolled in or undertaking a postgraduate degree in: Agriculture Biodiversity Ecology Animal Science Soil Science Or a related field Be a citizen of a Commonwealth country Have the right to live and work in the UK for the full internship duration ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Why Apply? This internship is ideal for young professionals seeking experience in: Climate change policy Sustainable land management Environmental research and governance International development and diplomacy Benefits include: Weekly stipend ( 498.60) Exposure to global climate and biodiversity initiatives Professional networking opportunities Experience within an international intergovernmental organisation ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); About the Commonwealth Secretariat The Commonwealth Secretariat is the intergovernmental organisation that supports member countries to achieve the Commonwealth s aims of development, democracy and peace. Members: are a voice for small and vulnerable states champion young people promote justice and human rights address threats like climate change, debt and inequality help grow economies and boost trade support decision-makers to make good laws and deliver policies help strengthen governance and build inclusive public institutions provide technical help and training send experts and observers to countries to give impartial advice and help solve national problems provide systems, software and research for managing resources. Commonwealth Member Countries (56): Antigua and Barbuda, Australia, Bahamas, Bangladesh, Barbados, Belize, Botswana, Brunei Darussalam, Cameroon, Canada, Cyprus, Dominica, Eswatini, Fiji, Gabon, Gambia, Ghana, Grenada, Guyana, India, Jamaica, Kenya, Kiribati, Lesotho, Malawi, Malaysia, Maldives, Malta, Mauritius, Mozambique, Namibia, Nauru, New Zealand, Nigeria, Pakistan, Papua New Guinea, Rwanda, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Saint Lucia, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Samoa, Seychelles, Sierra Leone, Singapore, Solomon Islands, South Africa, Sri Lanka, Tanzania, Togo, Tonga, Trinidad and Tobago, Tuvalu, Uganda, United Kingdom, Vanuatu, Zambia. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); How to Apply Interested candidates should submit: A CV A cover letter addressing key criteria in the person specification with clear examples Supporting documents include: Living Lands Charter Internship Job Description Application Guidelines Internship Policy APPLY NOW If you are passionate about climate action, biodiversity, and sustainable development, this is an excellent opportunity to gain real-world international experience while contributing to global environmental solutions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Early applications are strongly encouraged. The post Living Lands Charter Internships 2026 (x2) at the Commonwealth Secretariat – Paid Climate Change Internship in London appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Visiting PhD Fellowship 2026 at United Nations University-WIDER — Fully Funded Research Opportunity for PhD Students
summary: The UNU-WIDER Visiting PhD Fellowship Programme 2026 offers doctoral students an opportunity to strengthen their PhD research while working with one of the world s leading development economics institutes. This fully funded fellowship allows researchers to access UNU-WIDER s resources, collaborate with experts, and advance research focused on developing economies. For PhD candidates seeking international research exposure, publication opportunities, and academic networking, this programme provides a strong platform to enhance both dissertation quality and career development. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Visiting PhD fellows typically spend three consecutive months at UNU-WIDER before returning to their home institution. During the programme, fellows prepare one or more research papers and present a seminar on their research findings. They may also have the opportunity to publish their research in the WIDER Working Paper Series . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); You can read about previous experiences from the programme, here . About the UNU-WIDER Visiting PhD Fellowship Programme The Visiting PhD Fellowship Programme enables registered doctoral students to: Use UNU-WIDER s research resources and facilities Collaborate with researchers on shared research interests Develop research papers related to their dissertation or thesis Present a seminar based on their research findings Potentially publish in the WIDER Working Paper Series ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Fellows usually spend three consecutive months at UNU-WIDER before returning to their home institution. Fellowship Duration and Research Activities During the three-month fellowship period, participants are expected to: Advance their doctoral research Engage with UNU-WIDER researchers and academic discussions Prepare research outputs suitable for publication Present their research in a seminar setting The programme is designed to strengthen research quality and support policy-relevant academic work. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Be currently enrolled in a PhD programme Demonstrate ability to conduct research on developing economies Possess strong quantitative and/or qualitative analytical skills Be fluent in written and spoken English ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Although UNU-WIDER is an economics-focused institute, researchers from other social science disciplines may apply if their work aligns with development-related themes. Submissions on topics related to the UNU-WIDER work programme are particularly welcome. Priority Applicants Applications are especially encouraged from: Early-career researchers Female researchers Researchers from developing countries PhD candidates at later stages of their studies ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); The programme is highly competitive. In recent years, approximately one percent of applications were successful. Financial Support When the fellowship is undertaken in Helsinki, UNU-WIDER provides: A travel grant covering travel to and from the applicant s institution or country of residence ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Medical insurance during the fellowship period A monthly stipend of EUR 2,070 to support living expenses in Helsinki The programme does not cover expenses related to dependents. Application Timeline and Deadlines UNU-WIDER accepts applications twice each year. March Application Cycle Application form opens: 1 March (00:00 UTC+3) Deadline: 31 March (23:59 UTC+3) Fellowship period: September to November (same year) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); September Application Cycle Application form opens: 1 September (00:00 UTC+3) Deadline: 30 September (23:59 UTC+3) Fellowship period: March to June (following year) The online application link becomes active one month before each deadline. Application Requirements Applicants must submit: Completed online application form Updated curriculum vitae (CV) Reference letter from PhD supervisor The reference letter must be sent directly by the supervisor to: phdreference@wider.unu.edu This letter also confirms the applicant s enrolment in a PhD programme. Applications submitted by email or post are not accepted. Selection Process Applications are assessed competitively based on eligibility and research alignment Final decisions are communicated by email within four weeks after the deadline Selected applicants must confirm acceptance within two weeks of notification ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Why Apply for the UNU-WIDER Visiting PhD Fellowship? This fellowship is ideal for doctoral researchers who want to: Strengthen their dissertation with international research support Work closely with leading development economists Build academic and policy research networks Increase opportunities for publication Enhance future academic and professional prospects How to Apply Interested candidates should complete the online application form once it becomes available during the application window. If you are interested in participating in this programme you should complete and submit the application form. For more details on the submission and for practical information on the programme, please see our frequently asked questions page . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Contact Information If you have questions about the programme or you experience any problems with your online application, please first check our questions and answers page . If you are still experiencing problems please contact us at the following email address: phdfellow@wider.unu.edu Please note that replies may take up to five working days. The UNU-WIDER Visiting PhD Fellowship 2026 is a highly competitive and valuable opportunity for doctoral students conducting research on developing economies. With financial support, access to expert researchers, and opportunities to publish, the programme provides a strong environment for advancing high-quality, policy-relevant research. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); FIND OUT MORE AND APPLY HERE The Visiting PhD Fellowship Programme was previously known as the PhD Internship Programme. QUICK LINKS Learning Previous experiences Frequently asked questions Visiting PhD Fellowship The post Visiting PhD Fellowship 2026 at United Nations University-WIDER — Fully Funded Research Opportunity for PhD Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Carter Center Educational Programs 2026: Paid Internships and Graduate Assistantships (Complete Guide)
summary: Key Details Organization: The Carter Center Location: Atlanta, Georgia, USA (in-person) Programs: Paid Internships and Graduate Assistantships Focus Areas: Peace, Health, Democracy, Human Rights, International Development, Operations Internship Stipend: Spring (15 weeks): $5,100 Summer (10 weeks): $3,400 Fall (15 weeks): $5,100 Graduate Assistantship Compensation: $18.50 per hour 9 12 months, 20 hours per week Internship Deadlines: Summer 2026: March 2, 2026 (11:59 p.m. ET) Fall 2026: June 15, 2026 (11:59 p.m. ET) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Graduate Assistantship Deadlines: Application Opens: February 13, 2026 Application Deadline: April 23, 2026 (11:59 p.m. ET) Recommendation Letters Deadline: April 30, 2026 ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Who Can Apply: Undergraduate juniors or seniors Graduate students Recent graduates (within 24 months) International students authorized to work in the U.S. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Introduction The Carter Center Educational Programs provide structured professional opportunities for students and early-career professionals interested in advancing global peace, public health, democracy, and operational excellence. With more than four decades of experience and nearly 4,000 participants worldwide, the program has become a recognized pathway for individuals pursuing careers in international development, policy, and nonprofit leadership. Through paid internships and graduate assistantships, participants gain hands-on experience supporting real projects while building practical skills, professional networks, and career direction. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); About the Carter Center Educational Programs The Educational Programs were created to cultivate informed and committed leaders who can contribute meaningfully to global peace and health initiatives. Participants work alongside experts and program teams, applying academic knowledge to practical challenges while gaining exposure to the realities of international development work. The programs emphasize professional growth, mentorship, and experiential learning, helping participants explore career pathways and strengthen technical and leadership competencies. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Carter Center Internship Program Program Overview The Carter Center Internship Program offers diverse opportunities for students and recent graduates to gain professional experience across peace, health, and operational initiatives. Interns contribute directly to ongoing projects while developing skills relevant to careers in international affairs, nonprofit management, public health, and global policy. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet one of the following criteria: Undergraduate students who have completed at least two years of study (minimum 60 credit hours in the U.S. system) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Graduate students currently enrolled or enrolled within the last 24 months Recent graduates who completed their undergraduate degree within the past 24 months Additional requirements include: Authorization to work in the United States No citizenship restrictions International students enrolled at U.S.-based institutions may apply with approval from their host institution ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); All internship opportunities are based in Atlanta, Georgia. Internship Compensation Internships are paid through a stipend structure: Spring Semester (15 weeks): $5,100 Summer Semester (10 weeks): $3,400 Fall Semester (15 weeks): $5,100 Internship Program Areas Internship placements are available across three major divisions: Peace Programs China Focus (opens in new window) Conflict Resolution Program (opens in new window) Democracy (opens in new window) Human Rights Program (opens in new window) Inform Women, Transform Lives (opens in new window) Latin America and Caribbean Focus (opens in new window) Office of the Vice President of Peace (opens in new window) Rule of Law (opens in new window) Health Programs International Public Health (opens in new window) Mental Health Program (opens in new window) Special Health Projects (opens in new window) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Operations Programs Communications (opens in new window) Development (opens in new window) Educational Programs (opens in new window) People Operations Programs (Human Resources) (opens in new window) Information Technology (opens in new window) Strategy, Innovation and Learning (opens in new window) Volunteer Services/Art Collection (opens in new window) Overseas Operations (opens in new window) DEI Internal Communications Intern Experience and Career Impact Participants often describe the internship as a launching point for professional growth. Interns gain exposure to international development leadership, apply academic knowledge in real contexts, and build practical skills valued across the nonprofit and policy sectors. Former interns have contributed to projects related to election observation, governance, and regional initiatives while developing expertise that supports future graduate study and professional advancement. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Apply Today Start your journey at The Carter Center by applying for an internship or graduate assistantship today. Internship Application Carter Center Graduate Assistantship Program Program Overview The Graduate Assistantship Program offers a limited number of funded opportunities for master s and doctoral students seeking deeper engagement in research and program implementation. Graduate assistants work closely with program teams and contribute to substantive projects while balancing academic commitments. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Program Structure Duration: 9 12 months Workload: 20 hours per week Location: Atlanta, Georgia Compensation: $18.50 per hour Eligibility Requirements Applicants must: Be enrolled in a master s or doctoral program throughout the assistantship Have completed at least two semesters of graduate-level coursework at the start of the program Remain enrolled until the end of the assistantship ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Students who graduate before the assistantship concludes are not eligible. Graduate Assistantship Program Areas Peace Conflict Resolution (opens in new window) Democracy Program (opens in new window) Inform Women, Transform Lives (opens in new window) China Focus (opens in new window) Health Mental Health Program (opens in new window) Trachoma Control Program (opens in new window) Operations Development: Donor Landscape Analysis (opens in new window) People Operations Programs Apply Today Start your journey at The Carter Center by applying for an internship or graduate assistantship today. Graduate Assistantship Application Application Dates and Deadlines Internships Summer 2026 Session Application Deadline: March 2, 2026 (11:59 p.m. ET) Start Date: May 19, 2026 Fall 2026 Session Application Deadline: June 15, 2026 (11:59 p.m. ET) Start Date: August 25, 2026 Graduate Assistantships Application Opens: February 13, 2026 Application Deadline: April 23, 2026 (11:59 p.m. ET) Recommendation Letters Deadline: April 30, 2026 Why Apply to the Carter Center Educational Programs These programs are highly valuable for individuals seeking careers in international development and global impact. Key benefits include: Paid professional experience Exposure to peacebuilding and global health initiatives Opportunities to apply academic training in real-world contexts Access to experienced mentors and professionals Strong preparation for graduate study or careers in NGOs, policy, and international organizations ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); How to Apply and Contact Information Applications are submitted through the Carter Center Educational Programs portal. Candidates can apply for internships or graduate assistantships depending on eligibility. Apply Today Start your journey at The Carter Center by applying for an internship or graduate assistantship today. Internship Application Graduate Assistantship Application For questions and eligibility clarification: Email: EducationalPrograms@CarterCenter.org Conclusion The Carter Center Educational Programs offer a competitive and practical pathway for students and early-career professionals committed to advancing peace and health worldwide. With paid positions, structured mentorship, and meaningful project experience, the internships and graduate assistantships provide strong preparation for long-term careers in international development and global leadership. Applicants are encouraged to prepare early and submit strong, well-tailored applications before the deadlines. Source: https://www.cartercenter.org/educational-programs/#dates The post Carter Center Educational Programs 2026: Paid Internships and Graduate Assistantships (Complete Guide) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply Now: 2026 Internships at the U.S. Trade and Development Agency (USTDA)
summary: Are you looking to gain hands-on experience in global development, infrastructure, and international partnerships? The U.S. Trade and Development Agency (USTDA) is offering a range of internship opportunities for motivated students and young professionals eager to contribute to impactful projects in emerging markets. USTDA plays a key role as the U.S. government s first mover on critical infrastructure development , helping to advance shared strategic priorities while fostering sustainable economic growth worldwide. Through its internship program, you can work alongside experienced professionals and gain exposure to international development, trade, and policy implementation. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Available Internship Openings 1. Office of Programs Internship Application Period: February 19, 2026 March 20, 2026 Department: Office of Programs Overview: Interns in this office will support USTDA s core project development activities, contributing to initiatives that drive critical infrastructure development in emerging economies. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); 2. Intern, Office of Administration / Human Resources Application Period: February 19, 2026 March 20, 2026 Department: Office of Administration / HR Overview: This role offers experience in organizational operations, talent management, and HR systems that support the agency s global mission and internal workforce development. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); 3. Intern, Office of the Chief Information Officer Application Period: February 19, 2026 March 20, 2026 Department: Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO) Overview: Interns will assist with IT systems, digital infrastructure, and information management, supporting the technological backbone of USTDA s global operations. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Why Apply? An internship with USTDA offers you the chance to: Gain real-world experience in international development and trade Work on projects that support infrastructure growth in emerging markets Develop professional skills in policy, administration, technology, and project management Build a global professional network within a U.S. government agency Contribute to meaningful initiatives that drive economic development and sustainability Application Deadline All internship applications close on March 20, 2026 . Early applications are strongly encouraged. To apply to any of the internship opportunities, Visit Here Take the Next Step If you are passionate about global development, infrastructure, and international collaboration, these internships at the U.S. Trade and Development Agency provide a valuable platform to launch your career. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Prepare your application materials early, tailor your resume to the role you re applying for, and highlight your interest in international development and infrastructure. Start your journey toward a global career today. Discover more Internship opportunities Here.
